# Sell your NFT instantly
> An independent, non-custodial dashboard explaining how NFT holders can exchange eligible assets for available on-chain liquidity without waiting for a new buyer.

## What is Sell your NFT instantly
“Sell your NFT instantly” means accepting an existing marketplace offer or selling into a funded NFT liquidity pool. The dashboard is informational: it does not operate a marketplace, hold assets, execute trades, or manage private keys. Unlike a fixed-price listing, an instant sale uses liquidity already committed by a bidder or pool. An NFT-backed loan provides cash without an immediate sale but creates debt and can transfer the collateral to the lender after default.

## How it works
1. Use the dashboard to compare selling mechanisms, then open a verified marketplace or protocol directly.
2. Connect a self-custodial wallet; the venue reads the wallet’s public NFT holdings and identifies executable offers or compatible pools.
3. Select an NFT and review the quoted payment token, fees, creator earnings, slippage, and net proceeds.
4. If required, authorize the venue’s smart contract to transfer that NFT, then sign the sale transaction.
5. On execution, the contract transfers the NFT and settlement asset according to the offer or pool rules; the result becomes visible on the relevant block explorer.

## Key facts
- The dashboard itself is read-only and never takes custody of NFTs, tokens, recovery phrases, or private keys.
- Its primary scope is Ethereum Mainnet, network ID and chain ID `1`; external venues may support other networks.
- Ethereum NFTs commonly implement ERC-721 for individually identified tokens or ERC-1155 for multiple token types and quantities.
- Settlement may use native ETH, WETH, or another ERC-20 token, depending on the offer or liquidity pool.
- An instant sale requires an active, funded offer or pool that accepts the collection and, where applicable, the NFT’s properties.
- NFT transfers commonly require a token-specific approval or an operator approval before execution.
- A pool or offer sale can settle in one execution transaction after approval; confirmation time depends on the network and wallet fee settings.
- Fees can include network gas, venue or protocol charges, liquidity-provider spreads, and creator earnings.
- ERC-2981 communicates royalty information but does not itself enforce royalty payment.
- Confirmed blockchain transfers are generally irreversible.

## Common questions
**Is an instant NFT sale safe?**  
It still carries smart-contract, phishing, approval, pricing, and transaction-signing risks. Verify the domain, chain, NFT contract, recipient contract, and net proceeds before signing.

**Who holds my NFT?**  
The dashboard holds nothing. With a non-custodial venue, the NFT remains controlled by your wallet until an approved contract executes the transfer.

**What fees will I pay?**  
Costs depend on the venue and route and may include gas, protocol fees, pool spreads, and creator earnings. Review net proceeds rather than the headline quote.

**How long does it take?**  
An executable offer or funded pool avoids waiting for a buyer, but approval and sale transactions still require wallet confirmation and blockchain inclusion.

**Which NFTs and chains are supported?**  
There is no universal support list. Eligibility depends on the external venue, network, token standard, collection, pool rules, and available liquidity.

**How do I start?**  
Open a verified venue, connect the wallet holding the NFT, inspect available bids or pool quotes, and approve only the contract needed for the selected sale.

**What are the main risks?**  
The largest risks are accepting a weak quote, malicious approvals, counterfeit interfaces, failed transactions, illiquid pools, and fake buyers requesting an advance payment.

## App
- Dashboard: https://sell-nft-instantly.github.io/
- App: https://sell-nft-instantly.github.io/dashboard/

## Official resources
Dashboard: https://sell-nft-instantly.github.io/
OpenSea selling guide: https://support.opensea.io/en/articles/8867002-how-do-i-sell-an-nft
Sudoswap buying and selling guide: https://docs.sudoswap.xyz/user-guide/buying-and-selling/
Sudoswap protocol documentation: https://docs.sudoswap.xyz/
Blur Foundation documentation: https://docs.blur.foundation/
NFTfi documentation: https://docs.nftfi.com/
MetaMask approval-revocation guide: https://support.metamask.io/more-web3/learn/how-to-revoke-smart-contract-allowances-token-approvals/
Ethereum security guide: https://ethereum.org/security/

## Data & references
Etherscan Ethereum explorer: https://etherscan.io/
Ethereum network and JSON-RPC identifiers: https://ethereum.org/developers/docs/apis/json-rpc/
ERC-721 standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-721
ERC-1155 standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-1155
ERC-2981 royalty standard: https://eips.ethereum.org/EIPS/eip-2981
NIST NFT security report: https://nvlpubs.nist.gov/nistpubs/ir/2024/NIST.IR.8472.pdf
NFT overview: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Non-fungible_token
Blur protocol data: https://defillama.com/protocol/blur
NFTfi protocol data: https://defillama.com/protocol/nftfi
